---
title: "Volt Typhoon Analysis"
---

## Overview

This website presents an overview of Volt Typhoon activity, mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics, detection sources, and mitigations.

## Activity Timeline

This timeline shows how observed activity develops over time.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_activity_timeline.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>

## Detection Sources

This chart shows the ATT&CK detection data components most frequently associated with techniques attributed to Volt Typhoon. Each bar represents the number of ATT&CK detection references that rely on a specific telemetry source, such as process creation events, command execution logs, network traffic content, or user authentication records. Higher values indicate that a particular data source is relevant to detecting a larger portion of Volt Typhoon's documented techniques.
<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_detection_sources.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>
So what?
 - Volt Typhoon frequently uses legitimate administrative tools and valid credentials, making behavior-based monitoring more valuable than traditional antivirus signatures.
 - The telemetry most useful for detecting Volt Typhoon is generated by normal user and system activity—process creation, command execution, authentication events, and network communications.
 - Effective defense defense against Volt Typhoon depends less on signature-based malware detection and more on maintaining comprehensive visibility into endpoint, authentication, and network behavior.

## Mitigation Coverage

This chart shows which ATT&CK techniques have stronger or weaker mitigation coverage.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_mitigation_coverage.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>

## Tactic Coverage Heatmap

This heatmap shows how Volt Typhoon techniques are distributed across tactics.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_tactic_coverage_heatmap.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>
